question: The manufacturer testing data notes that your airplane experiences 450 lbs. of parasite drag at a normal cruise speed of 145 knots. You remember from your aerodynamics class that parasite drag increases with the square of an increase in airspeed. If you slow down to 60 knots, how much parasite drag is the airplane experiencing?
Your airplane has a 54" diameter propeller, and the normal cruise power setting is 2400 RPM. In cruise, how fast are the propeller tips traveling?
Your B-model Cessna 182 is indicating 147 mph in cruise at 4,500' (ISA). If your handheld GPS shows a ground speed of 124 knots, what headwind component are you experiencing?

You have been hired to do a flyover of a stadium with your vintage F-86 on a blistering 95°F summer day. You are orbiting at low altitude 5 nm from the stadium when you get the radio call marking 5 minutes until the flyover. If you are going to make the approach and pass at Mach 0.90, when should you depart your orbit point? Assume calm winds.
choice_a: 3 minutes, 52 seconds after the radio call
choice_b: 4 minutes, 11 seconds after the radio call
choice_c: 4 minutes, 31 seconds after the radio call
choice_d: 4 minutes, 47 seconds after the radio call
correct_choice: 4 minutes, 31 seconds after the radio call

question: You are following a standard 3° ILS glide slope for runway 17 (0' MSL). As you pass 3000', you know that the winds aloft are 140@16 kts, and your approach speed is 110 knots indicated. The OAT is showing 18°C, and the altimeter setting is 29.66". What descent rate should keep you on the glide slope?

Your airplane has a current ramp weight of 2089 lbs. and a CG of 44.9 inches. You are trying to load it to the aft CG limit for maximum aerodynamic efficiency. How much ballast should you add to the aft baggage compartment (sta. 95") to get the airplane to the aft CG limit of 47.3"?

As you break out of the clouds, you see the MALSR lighting system at the approach end of the runway. You remember from instrument ground school that the RAIL (runway alignment indicator light, i.e. "rabbit") is 800' long and the lights flash in sequence twice per second. How fast does the "rabbit" appear to move?

You are testing your new VOR/DME receiver, and you plan to depart the VOR on the 100 radial and fly to 25 DME. Reaching 25 DME, you will arc North and fly the 060 radial back to the VOR. If the winds are calm, and you are cruising at 118 KTAS, how long does it take to complete the test?

You are approaching your home airport on a 346 course. When you are 18 nm away, you hear your friend call up on the CTAF, and she reports her position as 13 nm and flying a 256 course. How far away from your friend are you?
How fast is an aircraft flying if it burned 512 fluid ounces of avgas at a rate of 42.4 liters per hour over 86 statute miles?
What is the density altitude for an airport elevation of 2,570' with temperature of 95°F and altimeter setting of 29.09" Hg?
What is an aircraft's descent rate in feet per second if it descends at 675 ft/sm at a ground speed of 135 knots?
How long will it take to fly 1,900 kilometers at Mach 0.62 with an OAT of -40°F?
Find ground speed under the following conditions: Wind is 070° at 18 knots, True Course is 216°, True Airspeed is 103 knots.
At -10°C, what is the pressure altitude if a pilot sees calibrated airspeed is 85 knots while the true airspeed is 83 knots?
What is the wind correction angle for an aircraft flying a heading of 189° at 129 knots ground speed if winds aloft are 305° at 41 knots and true airspeed is 106 knots?

What indicated airspeed should a pilot target if they wish to fly 450 NM in 3:30 at 7,500' MSL where winds are 150° at 18 knots, true course is 109°, temperature is -12°C, and altimeter setting is 30.27" Hg?
What is the minimum amount of AVGAS needed to fly 590 km at 185 knots including 0:45 minutes of reserve? Use an average fuel burn of 12.6 GPH.
Wind is 350° at 17 knots. On a 293° course and at 116 KTAS, what is the ground speed?
How many radials has an aircraft crossed while arcing east if the initial radial was 144° at 12 DME and the distance traveled on the arc is 4.8 NM?
Mach 0.77 at -38°C is equivalent to how many MPH?
What is the headwind component for Runway 16 with winds 122° at 14 knots?
If the cost of 100LL is $5.20 per gallon, how much will it cost to refuel your aircraft after burning 15 gallons on a cross country flight?
What climb rate is required if you are to maintain a climb gradient of 340 feet per nautical mile, while climbing at a true airspeed of 82 knots? Winds are calm.

You've decided to fulfill a longtime dream and fly into this year's AirVenture in Oshkosh, Wisconsin. You have faith in your single engine airplane, and have decided to fly across Lake Michigan rather than around it. Your course across the lake will be 285°, and the winds at your chosen altitude of 8,500 feet are 220° at 27 knots. Indicated airspeed is 105 knots. The temperature and pressure at 8,500 are both standard. Before you go any farther, if something goes wrong, how cold will the water below be in Fahrenheit if latest reports indicate a water temperature of 17°C?

Using the information from the previous question, and knowing your course across the lake will be 31 nautical miles, how far can you fly out over the lake before the time to continue equals the time to turn back?

question: Another student wishes to observe your training flight, so you agree to let them ride in the back. Your instructor asks you to calculate the weight and balance to make sure everything is within limits for the flight. If the weight and CG before the other student gets in is 2,018 pounds and 41.2 inches, what is the new weight and CG if the student weighs 190 pounds and sits at station 73?
On your most recent cross country, you covered 452 statute miles, while burning 170 liters of fuel at an average ground speed of 196 kilometers per hour. What was your average fuel burn?

The maximum allowable cross wind component for takeoff for your aircraft is 35 knots, including gusts. The maximum allowable tailwind is 10 knots. If the winds are from 180° at 35 knots, with gusts to 42 knots, which of the following runways is permitted for takeoff?
choice_a: None of these runways are legal. You'll need to wait for the weather to improve.
correct_choice: None of these runways are legal. You'll need to wait for the weather to improve.

question: You are descending on a 3° slope at 180 kts when ATC tells you to reduce to 150 kts. If you want to maintain the same 3° descent what rate of descent will you need to maintain?
Your aircraft is loaded to 2,480 lbs with a CG of 76.8". What will your new CG be if your 180 lb friend decides to join in the back seat at 108"?

Using the information from the previous question, the new CG puts you 0.91" behind the aft CG limit of your aircraft. How much weight must you move from the baggage compartment at 160" to the nose compartment at 26" to bring the CG 1" inside the aft limit?
context: Referenced question 32: Your aircraft is loaded to 2,480 lbs with a CG of 76.8". What will your new CG be if your 180 lb friend decides to join in the back seat at 108"?

question: You are flying a true course of 145 and a planned true airspeed of 112 kts. If the forecast winds aloft are 240@16 what magnetic heading should you fly? The variation is 12 West and the airplane has a +4 deviation error.
What would your planned groundspeed be in the above example?

question: If your airplane has a indicated never exceed speed of 160 kts what would that be in mach at 6,500' in ISA +13°C conditions?

While flying a cross country you decide to make ground speed checks by timing how long it takes to fly between radials. If you maintain 21 DME and cross the 137 radial at 1523z and the 177 radial at 1531z what is your ground speed?

Unfortunately, your navigation system seems to be malfunctioning, because at your 50 miles you find yourself 3 miles off left of course. If your original heading was 240° what heading would you need to fly to parallel your intended course?
If you still have 90 miles left to fly what total heading correction is required to fly directly to your destination?

question: Your home airport elevation is 1,600' but it's a slightly warm day at 35°C and standard pressure. What is the density altitude?
